title: Investigate + propose canonical fix for the analyst summary-write gap. monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt has been stale since 2026-04-25T08:04Z despite analyst running successfully many times. Two suspected bugs: (1) path typo monitor/analysis/ vs monitor/analyst/ in analyst-normal-analysis.md, and (2) summary write only documented in Normal mode dispatcher, missing from Modes 0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Tinker investigates the root cause across analyst.md + reference/analyst-*.md, designs the canonical fix (what to write, when to write, where to write), and proposes prompt edits.
context: Empirical observation 2026-05-14T05:00Z: latest-analysis-summary.txt mtime is 2026-04-25 08:04 UTC. Content of the file is from a 2026-04-25T07:04Z Normal-mode no-op run. That means analyst has run dozens of times since Apr 25 without overwriting the summary file.

Real work HAS been happening. Verified by mtime + completed_at scan on 2026-05-14T05:00Z: at least 20 tracker entries flipped to status=complete since 2026-05-13T12:00Z including EXP-329-WIN-057-AICBIC-defender-pivot, EXP-330-WIN-056-direct-import-post-hoc-correction, EXP-366-stale-tracker-drain-batch, EXP-326-WIN-021-tau-I-framing-close, and a large batch completed 16:06Z (EXP-325/327/331/338/339/340/341/344/345/346/347). 14 fresh issue-proposals also written. Two of the EXPs (329, 330) directly serviced baby's first-run escalations from PROP-034 Phase 1.

So analyst is healthy and producing work. The summary-write sentinel is broken, which hid analyst's state from the operator's pipeline-health dashboard and from tinker's pipeline-health audit (tinker uses 'Last output timestamp vs expected schedule' as the staleness check, and the broken sentinel makes analyst look paused since Apr 25).

Smoking gun 1 (path typo): /tmp/edit-clone/monitor/prompts/reference/analyst-normal-analysis.md line 7 and line 153 both reference 'monitor/analysis/latest-analysis-summary.txt' — note 'analysis' (with -is) directory, not 'analyst' (with -t). The actual workspace path everyone reads is monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt. If analyst is literally writing to monitor/analysis/, that's a different directory the rest of the pipeline doesn't watch. (Or, more likely, the analyst recognizes the typo and uses the workspace canonical path — but then there's no enforcement that the write actually happens.)

Smoking gun 2 (dispatcher gap): Step 7 'Write summary and update status' in analyst-normal-analysis.md is part of the Normal mode procedure. The Mode 0/1/1b/2/2b/3/4/5 dispatchers in analyst.md don't reference Step 7 — they have their own procedures (Mode 1 in analyst-mode1-expansions.md, Mode 2b in analyst.md inline, etc.). None of those dispatchers prescribe an explicit 'write summary' step at end-of-run. So when analyst runs Mode 1 (the dominant overnight case), it does the work but never updates latest-analysis-summary.txt.

Apr 25 last-write date matches the introduction of the workspace-canonical attention-inbox path (PROP-022 Phase 1 / FUSE source-of-truth shift). Hypothesis: the analyst.md edits that landed around then refactored the dispatcher to read inbox from FUSE, and in the refactor the Normal-mode 'Step 7 write summary' fell out of the per-mode procedures. Worth confirming via git log of analyst.md around 2026-04-25 to 2026-04-27.
Compare with curmudgeon's latest-review-summary.txt and decider's latest-decider-summary.txt — both update reliably every run. Whatever pattern they use, analyst should follow.
operator_intent: Tinker investigates the two suspected bugs (path typo + missing dispatcher hookup), confirms or refutes each via prompt-file analysis and git log review, identifies any third cause we missed, and proposes the canonical fix. The fix should ensure latest-analysis-summary.txt is overwritten EVERY analyst run (mode-agnostic), including no-op runs (so operator can see 'analyst ran 04:50Z, no work to do' rather than infer absence from a stale timestamp).

tasks.description: grep all of monitor/prompts/ for both 'monitor/analysis/' (with 'analysis') and 'monitor/analyst/' (with 'analyst') references. Tabulate which files use which. Identify any other directories that have a similar typo risk (e.g. monitor/integrity/ vs monitor/integrety/). Report each occurrence with file + line.
tasks.deliverable: A short tabulated list inside the PROP. Per-file count of 'analysis' vs 'analyst' references.

tasks.description: Read analyst.md + analyst-normal-analysis.md + analyst-mode0-onboarding.md + analyst-mode1-expansions.md + analyst-mode1b-predictions.md + analyst-mode34-procedures.md + analyst-mode5-prospective.md. For each, identify whether the mode procedure prescribes a summary write at end-of-run. List which modes have it and which don't. Confirm the hypothesis that only Normal mode does, and that all dispatcher modes lack it.
tasks.deliverable: Per-mode table: mode_name | writes_summary_at_end | path_used | conditional_or_unconditional.

tasks.description: git log monitor/prompts/analyst.md and the reference subdirectory, focusing on 2026-04-20 → 2026-04-30 window. Identify the commit (or commits) that broke the summary-write contract. Hypothesis: a refactor that introduced FUSE-canonical workspace paths for attention-inbox dropped the Normal-mode Step 7 from per-mode procedures.
tasks.deliverable: One or more candidate commit SHAs, with one-line description of what each touched. Conclude either 'confirmed: commit X introduced the gap' or 'not confirmed: gap predates 2026-04-25; the Normal-mode no-op pattern just happened to be the last code path that fired'.
tasks.fallback: If the git log doesn't surface a clear commit, search closed-issues.json + tinker reports for any historical mention of 'latest-analysis-summary' to see if this was discussed before.

tasks.description: Propose the canonical fix as a PROP-NNN. Options to evaluate:

design_space.option_A_dispatcher_postlude: Add a 'Write summary' postlude block to analyst.md that runs unconditionally at the end of every mode (and after every mode-switch in multi-mode runs). Path: monitor/analyst/latest-analysis-summary.txt. Content: per-run digest of which mode(s) fired, what was processed, what was deferred. Mirrors curmudgeon's latest-review-summary.txt pattern.
design_space.option_B_per_mode_step: Add an explicit 'Step N: Write summary' to each mode's reference file. More surface area but mode-specific summary content.
design_space.option_C_status_json_only: Drop the .txt summary file entirely and rely on status.json + the agent's actual outputs (EXPs, issue-proposals) as the dashboard signal. Tinker's pipeline-health audit would need to update its staleness rule accordingly.
design_space.option_D_hybrid: Option A + tinker pipeline-health audit explicitly checks both summary mtime AND substantive-output mtime (EXPs, issue-proposals, tracker writes), so a broken summary write can't silently hide a healthy agent.

design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Recommend one option with rationale. Operator preference unstated; default to the option that minimizes new failure modes.
Q2: For option A or B: what's the minimum content the summary must contain to be useful as a dashboard sentinel? Mode fired, items processed, next-run carry-over — what else?
Q3: For option D: should tinker's pipeline-health audit always cross-check substantive output mtimes even when the summary file IS fresh? (Defense against future similar bugs.)
Q4: Should the path typo fix be part of THIS PROP or a separate trivial fix? Recommendation: include here for atomicity.

Q5: Should the same audit be applied to baby's latest-baby-summary.txt? Baby's prompt has an explicit write step (analyst-baby.md step 10), but tinker's staleness check should still cover it. Confirm coverage.

tasks.description: Steve prefers honest evaluation. Steelman the case for NOT fixing this. Strongest counter-arguments to consider:
counter_args_to_address: 'Analyst is producing real work — the broken sentinel isn't hurting anything. Fix it only when something downstream actually breaks.' Rebuttal expected: the broken sentinel hides analyst state from operator situational awareness, masked Phase 1 split-analyst dynamics for ~18h, and is one of the prompt-correctness signals operators rely on to know whether to investigate.

'The mode dispatcher's no-op summary in Normal mode IS the correct pattern. The fix is to migrate Normal-mode style summaries to each mode, not add a postlude.' Rebuttal expected: postludes are less per-mode-surface and harder to forget; per-mode requires editing 7 reference files.
'Per-mode summaries lose information (mode-specific verbosity) when consolidated into a postlude.' Operator may need to decide on verbosity trade-off.

tasks.description: Author the PROP-NNN as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, then operator + tinker apply prompt edits.
tasks.deliverable: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-analyst-summary-write-canonical-fix.json with the full design, including the chosen option (with rationale), the verbatim prompt edits to apply, the path-typo fix, and the tinker-pipeline-health update.

in_scope_for_tinker: Read analyst.md, all reference/analyst-*.md, latest-analysis-summary.txt, status.json
git log analyst.md history (2026-04-20 to present)
Read curmudgeon.md and decider.md latest-summary patterns for comparison
Author one PROP-NNN file with the full design
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify analyst.md or any reference file directly. Propose-only.
Do NOT touch scheduling. Cadence is a separate concern.
Do NOT batch with PROP-035 (which is about clone-side disk archival, unrelated). Keep this PROP focused on the summary-write contract.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: Counter-argument section is mandatory (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too')
Recommend the most-conservative option that fixes the actual bug. Avoid scope creep into 'while we're here, redesign the dashboard'
Include exact verbatim prompt edits where applicable so operator can apply via copy-paste
